name: Haggis#2
recipeIngredient: 1 Sheep's stomach bag plus the pluck (lights, liver, and heart)
recipeInstructions:
1. Salt and pepper about ¼ pint beef stock.
2. Soak the stomach bag in salted water overnight.
3. Place the pluck (lights, liver, and heart) in a saucepan with the windpipe hanging over the edge. Cover with water and boil for 1½ hours. Impurities will pass out through the windpipe. Drain well and cool. Remove the windpipe and any gristle or skin.
4. Mince the liver and heart with the mutton (add some of the lights before mincing if desired).
5. Toast the oatmeal gently until pale golden brown and crisp.
6. Combine with minced mixture, suet, and onion. Season well and add sufficient stock to moisten well.
7. Pack into the stomach bag, filling it just over half-full as the stuffing will swell during cooking. Sew up the bag tightly or secure each end with string.
8. Put an upturned plate in the base of a saucepan of boiling water, stand the haggis on this and bring back to a boil. Prick the haggis all over with a large needle to avoid bursting and boil steadily for 3 to 4 hours.
9. Makes 6 to 8 servings.
